title: Glorious Golden Irish Cheese Gratin
description: A mustardy, glassy-jelly Irish dish featuring cheese slices, perfect for a date night, with a low-glycemic-friendly twist.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium saucepan, combine the milk, butter, and Dijon mustard, and heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the butter has melted and the mixture is hot but not boiling.
In a small bowl, mix the sodium alginate with a little water to make a smooth paste, then add it to the milk mixture, and stir to combine.
Arrange 6 slices of the Irish cheddar cheese in the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Pour the milk mixture over the cheese slices, and sprinkle with salt and pepper to taste.
Repeat the layers, starting with the remaining cheese slices, then the milk mixture, and finally a sprinkle of parsley.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil, and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the foil, and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the gratin is heated through.
Remove the dish from the oven, and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
